text.title: Edge Case Checklist — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for applying the edge-case checklist so screens don't ship with happy-path-only states.

rule.statement: The checklist contains 12 enumerated edge cases: (1) Empty (no data yet), (2) Empty-after-search (search returned nothing), (3) Long content (3x typical), (4) Very-short content, (5) Slow-network (data taking 3+ sec), (6) Offline, (7) Partial-load (some items loaded, others failed), (8) Error (HTTP 4xx / 5xx / network), (9) Permission-denied / unauthorized, (10) Rate-limited, (11) Dark mode (if supported), (12) RTL (if supported). Adding new items per-project is allowed; subtracting from the 12 requires an explicit in-scope-list reason.

rationale.source: NN/g empty-state research, Smashing Magazine real-world-browser studies

rule.rationale: The 12 items come from empirical post-release defect analysis — these are the states that ship broken because designers and AI defaults skip them. The enumerated list prevents "I'll think about edges later" — every item is named.

rule.statement: Every in-scope item MUST resolve to one of three: STATE-RENDERED (with link to the rendered state in the design file), N/A-WITH-REASON (one-sentence why the case doesn't apply here, e.g., "screen has no async data, no slow-network case"), or COPY-RULE-ONLY (when no separate render needed but copy / truncation / i18n rule applies, e.g., "long content → ellipsis after 60 chars + tooltip"). Items left null are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Null items are the silent killers — they ship as undefined behavior. Forcing one of three explicit resolutions prevents the gap.

rule.statement: For empty / long-content / error / partial-load items, the copy MUST be concrete: empty state has actual text + optional illustration ID + optional primary action; long-content has truncation rule (ellipsis at N chars, "show more" at N items, etc.); error has user-facing message (not the raw HTTP code or stack trace), recovery path, and support-contact if relevant. Lorem ipsum / "TBD" / "see Figma" is forbidden in the handoff.

rationale.source: NN/g error-message guidelines, FlowMapp UX-copy research

rule.rationale: Copy invented at dev time produces inconsistent UX (different empty-state messages across screens). Concrete copy at handoff time creates a copy library that scales across the product.

rule.statement: At project start, the team MUST declare which of the 12 items are in-scope for this product (e.g., "RTL: yes, we ship to Arabic markets"; "dark mode: yes; offline: yes; rate-limited: only for the admin dashboard"). The in-scope list is referenced by every screen's checklist. Forbidden: per-screen invented N/A reasons that the broader product would actually serve.
rule.rationale: Per-screen N/A reinvention creates inconsistency — one screen marks "RTL N/A" while a sibling renders RTL. Project-level in-scope list creates one source of truth; per-screen decisions are filtered against it.

rule.statement: Every STATE-RENDERED resolution MUST include a fetchable evidence link (Figma frame URL, Storybook story URL, or screenshot path). Every COPY-RULE-ONLY resolution MUST include the actual copy strings (not a "see copy file" handwave). Every N/A-WITH-REASON must include a one-sentence reason that references either the in-scope list OR a product-specific characteristic.
rule.rationale: Unverifiable resolutions devolve into unspecified-behavior. Evidence links keep the dev (and the QA, the AI codegen) anchored to actual design intent.

example.title: Resolution record for a list-of-orders screen
example.code:
Screen: /orders Product in-scope list: dark_mode=yes, RTL=no, offline=yes, rate-limited=yes (admin)
1. Empty: STATE-RENDERED Figma: /file/abc?node=orders-empty Copy: "No orders yet" + "Once you place an order, it appears here" Primary action: "Browse catalog" button.
2. Empty-after-search: STATE-RENDERED Figma: /file/abc?node=orders-empty-search Copy: "No orders match 'XYZ'" + "Try a different keyword or clear filters"
3. Long content: COPY-RULE-ONLY - Order ID: max 18 chars (none expected longer) - Customer name: ellipsis at 32 chars + tooltip on hover - Item names: wrap at 2 lines + ellipsis at 80 chars
4. Very-short content: N/A-WITH-REASON Schema enforces non-empty fields for orders; no very-short risk.
5. Slow-network: STATE-RENDERED Figma: /file/abc?node=orders-loading (skeleton 8 rows, no spinner)
6. Offline: STATE-RENDERED Figma: /file/abc?node=orders-offline Copy: "You're offline. Showing cached orders from {timestamp}."
7. Partial-load: STATE-RENDERED Figma: /file/abc?node=orders-partial Copy: "Some orders couldn't be loaded. Refresh to try again."
8. Error: STATE-RENDERED Figma: /file/abc?node=orders-error Copy: "Couldn't load orders. {error_id} — Refresh or contact support."
9. Permission-denied: N/A-WITH-REASON Logged-in users always see their own orders; route-level guard handles the auth case at a different layer.
10. Rate-limited: COPY-RULE-ONLY In admin context only: "Too many requests. Slowing down…" toast. Auto-retry with backoff.
11. Dark mode: COPY-RULE-ONLY (uses token-based theming, no separate frame)
12. RTL: N/A-WITH-REASON (product in-scope list excludes RTL)
example.note: Illustrates r1-r5: full 12-item, in-scope list referenced, evidence per item, concrete copy.

example.title: Happy-path-only handoff
example.code:
Screen: /orders Edge cases: "we'll figure out empty / error states later"
Outcome: - Empty state: dev invented "No orders" with no recovery action. - Error state: dev showed raw 500 error message. - Slow load: no spinner, page looked broken for 4 sec. - Offline: page showed blank. - Dark mode: dev used inline colors (theme broken).
Customer support: 12 tickets in first week about "broken orders page".
Repair: enforce r1, r2, r3. Resolve all 12 items at handoff time; "later" is not a resolution.
example.note: Illustrates r1, r2, r3 violations — the standard "ship without edge cases" failure.
